subtopics.label: Dense Fog Advisories on Great Lakes
subtopics.summary: This cluster is focused on new weather advisories for dense fog impacting navigation on the Great Lakes. Alerts describe dense fog reducing visibility to various ranges, such as less than 1 NM [3leae6r3xsv2g] or 1/4 to 1/2 NM [3lejbqmqfgz2y]. These advisories specifically mention the Southern Open Waters of Lake Michigan [3leae6r3xsv2g, 3ldgqy4ziuh2b] and Portions of Lake Superior [3lejbqmqfgz2y], with a consistent warning that low visibility will make navigation difficult [3leae6r3xsv2g, 3ldgqy4ziuh2b, 3lejbqmqfgz2y].

key_quotes.quote: NEW WEATHER ADVISORY: Dense Fog Advisory
key_quotes.note: This quote directly identifies the type of official alert being shared across the cluster.

key_quotes.quote: Dense fog will reduce visibility to 1/4 to 1/2 NM.
key_quotes.note: This highlights the core environmental condition and its specific, measured impact on visibility.

key_quotes.quote: IMPACTS...Low visibility will make navigation difficult.
key_quotes.note: This quote succinctly states the primary concern and practical danger addressed by these advisories.

subtopics.label: Observing Sea Smoke on Lake Superior
subtopics.summary: Discussions in this cluster focus on the natural phenomenon of sea smoke occurring on Lake Superior [3m7xk7p6b7c2v, 3md3tu6ku222u, 3lg53jijbrs2x]. Users express awe at its intensity, describing it as "out of control" or "off the charts" [3m7xk7p6b7c2v, 3md3tu6ku222u], particularly when observed in the morning [3lg53jijbrs2x].

key_quotes.quote: Sea smoke on Lake Superior is out of control
key_quotes.note: This quote vividly describes the perceived extreme intensity of the sea smoke.

key_quotes.quote: Amazing to see the Sea Smoke on Lake Superior this morning.
key_quotes.note: This highlights the sense of wonder and the specific context of morning observation.

key_quotes.quote: the sea smoke 💨 on Lake Superior is off the charts today!!
key_quotes.note: This phrase underscores the extraordinary scale and impressive nature of the sea smoke observed.

subtopics.label: Observations of Clouds Over the Great Lakes
subtopics.summary: This cluster discusses observations of various cloud formations over the Great Lakes, including specific mentions of Lake Michigan [3mo2kglifq22a] and Lake Erie [3lpkdkxfeec2h]. Users share experiences such as seeing 'crazy clouds' [3mo2kglifq22a] or observing them while flying to Chicago [3klqatu7qpk2a]. There's also an anecdotal account of identifying snow and rain clouds by their color, passed down through family tradition near Lake Erie [3lpkdkxfeec2h].

key_quotes.quote: some crazy clouds over Lake Michigan currently.
key_quotes.note: This quote directly captures a user's real-time observation and description of clouds over one of the Great Lakes.

key_quotes.quote: Clouds over the Great Lakes (I was flying to Chicago in October 2022)
key_quotes.note: This represents observations made during travel, providing context for how some users encounter and share cloud sightings.

key_quotes.quote: clouds with a bluish tint were snow clouds and grey clouds were rain clouds.
key_quotes.note: This highlights the sharing of traditional or anecdotal knowledge about cloud types and their associated weather, passed down through generations.
